roadmap.goal: Fix ADO Bug 5016 — Cache<TKey,TValue> in Lighthouse.Backend/Cache/Cache.cs uses a non-thread-safe Dictionary<,>, causing IndexOutOfRangeException in BaseMetricsService.MetricsCache under concurrent metric requests (836 occurrences in 15h in customer Liz's instance). Fix is a one-line swap of the backing store to ConcurrentDictionary<,> with TryGetValue/TryRemove in Get/Remove, preserving the public surface and lazy-expiry-on-read semantics. Acceptance: the 7 scenarios in docs/feature/bug-5016-cache-thread-safety/acceptance/bug-5016-cache-thread-safety.feature go GREEN; the full backend test suite continues to pass; Stryker mutation gate on Cache.cs is >=80%.

validation.notes: Two-step roadmap reflects the actual scope: write RED regression tests, then make GREEN with ConcurrentDictionary swap. No architect dispatch — the design IS the one-line fix already prescribed in ADO 5016 repro steps and validated against existing ConcurrentDictionary precedent at docs/product/architecture/brief.md:222 (OAuth single-flight uses ConcurrentDictionary<int, SemaphoreSlim>). Sentinel approved the DISTILL artifacts upstream with zero blockers.

out_of_scope: Broader cache redesign (eviction policy, size cap, telemetry, distributed cache) — tracked under epic #4618.
Single-flight semantics (preventing two threads from recomputing the same expired metric simultaneously) — separate feature if desired; not in scope for crash fix.
Removing the BaseMetricsService.MetricsCache static lifetime — orthogonal to the thread-safety bug.
Changing the lazy-expiry-on-read behaviour of Get — preserved verbatim so non-concurrent callers see no behavioural drift.

phases.name: Surgical thread-safety fix on Cache<,>
phases.description: Two-step RED -> GREEN: (01-01) author CacheTest.cs with 5 concurrency repros + 2 parity guards mapped 1:1 from the .feature scenarios; verify the concurrency tests fail RED on today's Dictionary-backed Cache (IndexOutOfRangeException / InvalidOperationException) and the parity tests already pass GREEN; (01-02) swap Cache.cs's backing store to ConcurrentDictionary and adjust Get/Remove to TryGetValue/TryRemove; verify all 7 CacheTest scenarios go GREEN and the full backend test suite remains green.

steps.name: Author RED CacheTest.cs (NUnit, 7 scenarios mirroring the .feature)
steps.description: Create new directory Lighthouse.Backend/Lighthouse.Backend.Tests/Cache/ and new file CacheTest.cs inside it. Implement 7 NUnit [Test] methods mapping 1:1 onto the scenarios in docs/feature/bug-5016-cache-thread-safety/acceptance/bug-5016-cache-thread-safety.feature. Use Parallel.For / Task.WhenAll with a starting Barrier to maximise race probability on the concurrent scenarios (target reliable RED in < 2s on the standard CI runner; hard timeout 5s). Use Task.Delay or short Thread.Sleep past the expiry window for expiry-driven scenarios. Tests must exercise the *public* surface only: Store / Get / Remove / Keys.
criteria: CacheTest.cs is a new file under a new Lighthouse.Backend.Tests/Cache/ directory mirroring the production layout Lighthouse.Backend/Cache/Cache.cs.
7 [Test] methods are present, each named after the scenario it implements (PascalCase, descriptive — never Test_X_Y).
Concurrency tests use Task.WhenAll or Parallel.For with a starting Barrier so threads launch simultaneously (maximise race window).

When run against the current Dictionary-backed Cache.cs, the 5 concurrency tests FAIL with IndexOutOfRangeException or InvalidOperationException ("Collection was modified"); the 2 parity tests PASS. Confirm by running `dotnet test --filter "FullyQualifiedName~CacheTest"` and capturing the output.
No banned comments (no // ===== dividers, no // Arrange / Act / Assert labels, no // Given/When/Then narration, no provenance comments). Test method names + helper names carry the meaning.
Each test has a hard timeout (NUnit [Timeout(5000)] or equivalent) to prevent CI hangs if the thread-safety guarantee regresses.
Commit message: `test(cache): add bug-5016 regression suite for Cache<,> thread-safety`.

steps.name: Swap Cache<,> backing store to ConcurrentDictionary
steps.description: Edit Lighthouse.Backend/Lighthouse.Backend/Cache/Cache.cs. Replace `Dictionary<TKey, CacheItem<TValue>>` with `ConcurrentDictionary<TKey, CacheItem<TValue>>` (using System.Collections.Concurrent). Rewrite Get to use TryGetValue + TryRemove on lazy-expiry. Rewrite Remove to use TryRemove. Store stays as `cache[key] = ...` (ConcurrentDictionary supports the indexer atomically). Keys getter unchanged (delegates to ConcurrentDictionary.Keys which returns a snapshot). Verify GREEN end-to-end with full backend test suite.
steps.scenario_name: All 7 CacheTest scenarios + full backend suite
red_to_green_tests: Full backend test suite (`dotnet test Lighthouse.Backend/Lighthouse.Backend.Tests/`) stays GREEN
criteria: Cache.cs uses `private readonly ConcurrentDictionary<TKey, CacheItem<TValue>> cache = new();` (no other backing store).
Get uses `cache.TryGetValue(key, out var cached)` and `cache.TryRemove(key, out _)` on expiry.
Store keeps the indexer assignment (ConcurrentDictionary indexer is atomic).
Public surface unchanged — no signature edits to Store / Get / Remove / Keys; no new public method.
All 7 CacheTest scenarios pass. Confirm by running `dotnet test --filter "FullyQualifiedName~CacheTest"` and capturing the output.
Full `dotnet test` for Lighthouse.Backend.Tests still passes (no regression in BaseMetricsService / WorkTrackingConnectorFactory / GitHubService).
No banned comments added to Cache.cs.
Commit message: `fix(cache): use ConcurrentDictionary to prevent IndexOutOfRangeException under concurrent metric requests (ADO bug 5016)`.
